stem: An engineer takes 15 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 46.9 % and a standard deviation of 4.5 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically underestimates the true value by between 4.4% and 6.8%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 46.9 %. After correcting for the 5.6% bias: ≈ 49.7 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.4%-6.8%) and sampling variability: [46.8, 52.6]. Point estimate: 49.7 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
